mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening aggregate demand that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in supply that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% and a rise in the consumer price index above 250 signals a monetary easing cycle that increases aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% and CPIAUCSL > 250 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the employment in the manufacturing sector above 15 million signals a strengthening labor market that increases aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 15M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the real GDP below 17000 signals a weakening aggregate demand that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 17000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the real sales of retail trade above 480 million signals an increase in supply that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 480M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
